file.description: Main theme stylesheet with theme header and custom styles for the Poured Stone and Glass aesthetic.
Central theme configuration with the Poured Stone and Glass palette, Playfair Display + Jost typography, and architectural spacing.

file.content:
<?php
require_once __DIR__ . '/styles.php';
require_once __DIR__ . '/fonts.php';
require_once __DIR__ . '/theme-assets-rewrite.php';

if ( ! function_exists( 'concrete_and_light_setup' ) ) {
    function concrete_and_light_setup() {
        add_theme_support( 'wp-block-styles' );
        add_theme_support( 'editor-styles' );
        add_theme_support( 'responsive-embeds' );
    }
}
add_action( 'after_setup_theme', 'concrete_and_light_setup' );

file.description: Enqueue Google Fonts for Playfair Display and Jost.
file.content:
<?php
if ( ! function_exists( 'concrete_and_light_fonts' ) ) {
    function concrete_and_light_fonts() {
        wp_enqueue_style(
            'concrete-and-light-fonts',
            'https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@200;300;400;500&family=Playfair+Display:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap',
            array(),
            null
        );
    }
}
add_action( 'enqueue_block_assets', 'concrete_and_light_fonts' );

file.description: Site header with brand name and navigation — minimal, architectural aesthetic.
Site footer with contact info, navigation columns, and credit line.
file.content:
Concrete & Light

Spaces carved from silence and poured in stone. An architectural practice dedicated to the honest expression of material and light.

Studio

Projects
Practice
Materials
Publications

Connect

Instagram
LinkedIn
Archdaily
Newsletter

Visit

47 Stone Quarter
London EC2A 3QR
United Kingdom

14th Floor, Maker Maxity
Bandra Kurla Complex
Mumbai 400051, India

2026 Concrete & Light. All rights reserved.

Created with Telex, powered by WordPress

file.description: Main landing page template — hero cover, philosophy section, featured projects, materials showcase, testimonial, and contact CTA.
file.content:
Concrete
& Light

Spaces carved from silence and poured in stone.

Philosophy

Architecture as an act of listening

We believe that great architecture begins with restraint. Each project is an investigation into how light, material, and human presence can coexist in honest relationship. We do not decorate. We reveal.

Our practice spans private residences, cultural institutions, and public spaces across three continents. What unites them is a commitment to material truth and spatial generosity — the conviction that a building should give more than it takes.

27

Years of practice

84

Completed works

12

Countries

Selected Projects

Recent works

View all projects

Cultural / Mumbai / 2024

Bandra Arts Pavilion

A 2,800 sqm cultural pavilion on Bandra's waterfront. Stepped concrete terraces open toward the Arabian Sea, channeling monsoon breezes through deep-set corridors.

Residential / Mumbai / 2024

Worli Horizon Tower

A 32-storey luxury residence in Worli. Deep concrete louvres modulate the equatorial sun while framing panoramic views of the sea link and the Mumbai harbour.

Gallery / Mumbai / 2023

The Colaba Vault

A heritage restoration and gallery insertion in South Mumbai. New concrete volumes thread through colonial-era stone arches, creating a dialogue between centuries.

Materials

Honest substance

Board-formed Concrete

Each pour carries the memory of the formwork. We specify local timber species to imprint regional character into every surface — a geological fingerprint unique to each building.

Structural Glass

Light is our most essential material. We work with specialist fabricators to engineer glass systems that dissolve the boundary between interior volume and exterior landscape.

Natural Stone

We source stone from quarries within proximity of each site. Limestone, basalt, and sandstone connect our buildings to the deep time of their landscape — anchoring architecture in geological truth.

Voices from Mumbai

What our clients say

Concrete & Light understood what Mumbai demands of its architecture — resilience against the monsoon, grace under the tropical sun. Our tower in Worli breathes with the city.

Arjun Mehta, Worli Horizon Tower

They listened to the heritage of Colaba before drawing a single line. The result is a gallery where Victorian stone and raw concrete exist in a conversation that honours both epochs.

Priya Kapoor, The Colaba Vault

The Bandra Pavilion has become the cultural heartbeat of our neighbourhood. People gather on the concrete terraces at sunset as naturally as the tide meets the shore.

Rohan Deshmukh, Bandra Arts Pavilion

Begin a conversation

Every structure begins with a question.
What is yours?

We welcome conversations about projects of any scale. Reach out to discuss how we might bring your vision into concrete form.

Get in touch

studio@concreteandlight.com

Heritage Practice

Restoration & Adaptive Reuse

Heritage restoration at Concrete & Light is not preservation for its own sake. It is a conversation between centuries — an act of careful listening to the original structure before introducing contemporary interventions that serve present-day needs while honouring the building's memory.

Our approach is rooted in material honesty: new concrete volumes are left raw and unfinished to clearly distinguish them from the original stone and plaster, creating a legible timeline within each space.

A restored building should reveal its full history — not erase half of it.

Founding Principle

Our Restoration Process

01

Archaeological Survey

Before any design begins, we conduct a thorough archaeological and structural survey. We map every material layer, document original construction techniques, and identify the building's spatial DNA.

02

Sensitive Intervention

New elements are designed to be clearly legible as contemporary additions. Raw concrete, steel, and glass form a distinct material vocabulary that complements without mimicking the original fabric.

03

Living Continuity

The restored building must serve its community today. We programme flexible spaces that invite new uses while preserving the spatial generosity and atmospheric quality of the original architecture.

Featured Heritage Project

The Colaba Vault

Gallery / Mumbai / 2023

Originally built in 1887 as a merchant's warehouse in South Mumbai, The Colaba Vault lay dormant for decades before Concrete & Light was commissioned to transform it into a world-class contemporary art gallery.

New board-formed concrete walls were threaded through the original stone arches, creating a sequence of intimate gallery rooms within the expansive warehouse shell. Zenithal skylights replace the deteriorated roof structure, flooding each space with the diffused tropical light that Mumbai offers so generously.

The project received the UNESCO Asia-Pacific Award for Cultural Heritage Conservation in 2024, recognising the sensitive balance between preservation and contemporary architectural expression.

<!-- wp:template-part {"slug":"header","area":"header"} /-->
<!-- wp:group {"tagName":"main","backgroundColor":"charcoal","align":"full","style":{"spacing":{"padding":{"top":"0","bottom":"0"},"margin":{"top":"0"}}},"layout":{"type":"constrained","wideSize":"1280px"}} --> <main class="wp-block-group alignfull has-charcoal-background-color has-background" style="margin-top:0;padding-top:0;padding-bottom:0">
<!-- wp:post-content {"align":"wide","layout":{"type":"constrained","wideSize":"1280px"}} /-->
</main> <!-- /wp:group -->
<!-- wp:template-part {"slug":"footer","area":"footer"} /-->

Archive

No projects found. Check back soon for new work.

<?php

/**
 * Rewrites theme:./ URLs in block content.
 * It allows theme asset relative paths in templates e.g. theme:./assets/image.png
**/

// Rewrites theme:./ URLs of block content in the frontend.
add_filter( 'render_block', function ( $content ) {
	if ( ! $content ) {
		return $content;
	}
	$base = get_stylesheet_directory_uri();
	$content = preg_replace( '/(src|href)=(["\']?)theme:\.\//', '$1=$2' . $base . '/', $content );
	$content = preg_replace( '/url\((["\']?)theme:\.\//', 'url($1' . $base . '/', $content );
	return $content;
} );

// Enqueues script for theme:./ URL rewriting in the editor.
add_action( 'enqueue_block_editor_assets', function () {
	wp_enqueue_script( 'theme-assets-editor-rewrite', get_stylesheet_directory_uri() . '/theme-assets-editor-rewrite.js', [ 'wp-hooks', 'wp-compose', 'wp-element' ], '1.0.0', true );
	wp_add_inline_script( 'theme-assets-editor-rewrite', 'window.THEME_ASSETS_BASE_URL=' . wp_json_encode( get_stylesheet_directory_uri() ) . ';', 'before' );
} );

<?php

/**
 * Theme styles and editor styles setup.
 * It enqueues the main stylesheet in the front-end and in the editor.
**/

add_action( 'after_setup_theme', function () {
	add_theme_support( 'editor-styles' );
	add_editor_style( 'style.css' );
} );

add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', function () {
	wp_enqueue_style( 'telex-theme-style', get_stylesheet_uri(), [], wp_get_theme()->get( 'Version' ) );
} );
